components.name: AurumButton
components.summary: Aurum's primary button component.
components.signature:
@Composable
fun AurumButton(
    label: String,
    onClick: () -> Unit,
    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
    variant: AurumButtonVariant = AurumButtonVariant.Primary,
    size: AurumButtonSize = AurumButtonSize.Medium,
    isFullWidth: Boolean = false,
    isLoading: Boolean = false,
    progress: Float? = null,
    leading: (@Composable () -> Unit)? = null,
    trailing: (@Composable () -> Unit)? = null,
    enabled: Boolean = true,
)

params.name: label
params.doc: the button label text

params.name: onClick
params.doc: called when the button is clicked

params.name: modifier
params.doc: the [Modifier] to apply

params.name: variant
params.doc: the visual style variant -- defaults to [AurumButtonVariant.Primary]

params.name: size
params.doc: the size variant -- defaults to [AurumButtonSize.Medium]

params.name: isFullWidth
params.doc: whether the button fills its parent width -- defaults to `false`

params.name: isLoading
params.doc: whether to show a loading spinner in place of the label -- defaults to `false`

params.name: progress
params.doc: optional auto-advance fill in `0f..1f` — when non-null the button paints a faded-primary track with a primary fill that grows to this fraction and dims the label; defaults to `null`

params.name: leading
params.doc: optional composable slot rendered before the label (e.g. an icon)

params.name: trailing
params.doc: optional composable slot rendered after the label (e.g. an icon)

params.name: enabled
params.doc: whether the button is interactive -- defaults to `true`

components.intendedUse: Use when the user is performing a deliberate, named action (submit, confirm, continue, retry). One [AurumButtonVariant.Primary] per surface — pair Primary with Secondary or Tertiary for additional actions. Prefer [AurumLinkButton] for low-emphasis inline actions and [AurumIconButton] for icon-only top-bar slots.

do: Use one [AurumButtonVariant.Primary] per screen for the dominant CTA.
Choose [AurumButtonSize.Medium] as the default; [Large] only for full-width hero CTAs; [Small] for inline / compact rows.
Set `isLoading = true` synchronously when the action starts a network round-trip — don't render a separate spinner alongside.
Drive `progress` from a countdown for an auto-advancing CTA — the fill mirrors the time left before the action fires on its own.
dont: Stack two `Primary` buttons next to each other — pair `Primary` with `Secondary`.
Use a button to navigate without a side-effect; use [AurumLinkButton] or a plain tap target.
Pass `Modifier.fillMaxWidth()` — use `isFullWidth = true` so the Aurum padding / touch-target rules apply.

params.name: variant

params.name: size
params.doc: the size variant -- defaults to [AurumButtonSize.Medium]

params.name: isFullWidth
params.doc: whether the button fills its parent width -- defaults to `false`

params.name: isLoading
params.doc: whether to show a loading spinner in place of the label -- defaults to `false`

params.name: progress
params.doc: optional auto-advance fill in `0f..1f` — when non-null the button paints a faded-primary track with a primary fill that grows to this fraction and dims the label; defaults to `null`

params.name: leading
params.doc: optional composable slot rendered before the label (e.g. an icon)

params.name: trailing

params.name: enabled

ios.kdoc:
Aurum's primary button component — the SwiftUI port of aurum-android's
`AurumButton`.

A custom primitive (`Button` + a private `ButtonStyle` for
`configuration.isPressed`) — not a restyled system button — so every
state matches the Figma component exactly and bespoke interactions
(the dimensional hard-shadow press) are unconstrained. Features consume
this view for any button.

Three visual `variant` variants (`AurumButtonVariant`) and three `size`
variants (`AurumButtonSize`) are supported. Optional `leading` and
`trailing` slots accept icon views for icon-and-label buttons; slot
content picks up the button's foreground via `\.aurumContentColor`.

Platform notes vs Android: the ripple's place is taken by a 12%
content-colour press overlay (except the dimensional full-width primary,
whose press cue is purely the shadow drop + 2 pt sink); the minimum
touch target reserves 44 pt (HIG) rather than Android's 48 dp.

components.name: AurumIconButton
components.summary: Aurum's icon-only button primitive.
components.signature:
@Composable
fun AurumIconButton(
    onClick: () -> Unit,
    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
    enabled: Boolean = true,
    content: @Composable () -> Unit,
)

params.name: onClick
params.doc: called when the button is clicked

params.name: modifier
params.doc: the [Modifier] to apply

params.name: enabled
params.doc: whether the button is interactive — defaults to `true`

params.name: content
params.doc: the composable content — typically an [AurumIcon]

components.intendedUse: Use as a top-bar action affordance, dense list-row trailing action, or any spot where a labelled button would crowd the layout. The 48 dp touch target is maintained even when the visible glyph is smaller. For label-bearing actions reach for [AurumButton]; for low-emphasis text actions use [AurumLinkButton].

do: Provide an explicit `contentDescription` on the inner [AurumIcon] — icon-only buttons fail accessibility audits without one.
Use as the trailing-icon slot inside `AurumPageHeaderAction` / list rows where space is constrained.
Pick monosemic icons (close, more, edit) — never decorative ones whose meaning shifts by context.
dont: Use as the screen's primary CTA — icon-only buttons read as utility, not commitment; use [AurumButton] for that.
Cluster more than 2 [AurumIconButton]s in a row without grouping under an `AurumPageHeader` overflow — three is overflow territory.
Wrap `material3.IconButton` directly in feature code; that's exactly what this component prevents.

ios.kdoc:
Aurum's icon-only button primitive. The SwiftUI port of aurum-android's
`AurumIconButton`.

Guarantees a 44 pt minimum touch target (the HIG floor; Android's M3
wrapper reserves 48 dp) even when the visible glyph is smaller. The
`content` slot typically renders an `AurumIcon`; the caller controls the
icon size and tint via `AurumIcon`'s parameters. Pressed dims the glyph —
the analogue of M3's ripple for an icon-only affordance.

components.name: AurumLinkButton
components.summary: Aurum's link button -- a text-only button with no background or border.
components.signature:
@Composable
fun AurumLinkButton(
    label: String,
    onClick: () -> Unit,
    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
    leading: (@Composable () -> Unit)? = null,
    trailing: (@Composable () -> Unit)? = null,
    enabled: Boolean = true,
)

params.name: label
params.doc: the link label text

params.name: onClick
params.doc: called when the link is clicked

params.name: modifier
params.doc: the [Modifier] to apply

params.name: leading
params.doc: optional composable slot rendered before the label (e.g. an icon)

params.name: trailing
params.doc: optional composable slot rendered after the label (e.g. an icon)

params.name: enabled
params.doc: whether the link is interactive -- defaults to `true`

components.intendedUse: Use for low-emphasis inline actions next to body copy ("Skip", "Read more", "Resend OTP", "Forgot password?"). The lack of fill / outline makes it read as text — not as a CTA — so users feel free to ignore it. For deliberate commitment actions (submit, confirm, continue) reach for [AurumButton]; for icon-only affordances use [AurumIconButton].

do: Pair with longer prose context — "Already have an account? Sign in" reads naturally; a bare standalone link button looks orphaned.
Reserve for navigational + secondary actions — "Forgot password?", "Resend code", "Learn more".
Place inline with the surrounding paragraph copy at the same baseline; don't right-align it as a fake CTA.
dont: Use as the form-submit button — that's [AurumButton] with `Primary` variant; users won't perceive the link as a commitment.
Stack two link buttons in the same row — they read as a list of links, losing the "single low-emphasis action" intent.
Render destructive actions (Delete, Cancel subscription) here — destructive flows need [AurumButton] with explicit confirmation, not text-styled affordances.

ios.kdoc:
Aurum's link button — a text-only button with no background or border.
The SwiftUI port of aurum-android's `AurumLinkButton`.

Per the Figma spec this is a separate component from `AurumButton`:
brand-purple bold text with no fill or outline, fixed 56 pt height.
Pressed dims the label to the subtle brand colour (Android's pressed
state); there is no other press feedback, matching Android's
`indication = null`.

components.name: AurumTransactionListCard
components.summary: A status-tinted transaction row for payment / withdrawal / savings history lists.
components.signature:
@Composable
fun AurumTransactionListCard(
    label: String,
    dateText: String,
    amount: String,
    suffixText: String?,
    status: AurumTransactionStatus,
    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
    helperText: String? = null,
    leading: (@Composable () -> Unit)? = null,
)

params.name: label
params.doc: transaction label; ellipsises past one line

params.name: dateText
params.doc: pre-formatted date (e.g. `"18 Sep • 7:00 pm"`)

params.name: amount
params.doc: pre-formatted amount (e.g. `"₹ 100"`)

params.name: suffixText
params.doc: optional gold-weight suffix (e.g. `"0.0045 gms"`); `null` to hide

params.name: status
params.doc: validation state — drives wash, badge icon, and amount/helper colour

params.name: modifier
params.doc: the [Modifier] to apply

params.name: helperText
params.doc: explanatory text below the inner card in the status colour; `null` to hide

params.name: leading
params.doc: optional 40 dp slot for the row's leading visual — caller provides

components.intendedUse: Use as a single transaction row inside a scrollable history list — savings, withdrawals, gold buy / sell, referral payouts. For a single "this just happened" callout use the Snackbar / Banner family; for a full transaction-detail screen use the bespoke detail layout.

do: Pre-format `dateText`, `amount`, and `suffixText` at the call site with locale-aware utilities — the composable takes strings only.
Drive `status` from real backend state (Success / Error / Pending); wash + badge + amount colour all flow from it.
Pass `helperText` on failed / pending rows for inline context; leave it `null` for happy-path Success rows.
dont: Pass raw `Date` / `BigDecimal` / `Money` through `toString()` — locale-broken output ships the moment a user switches device language.
Recolour the surface, badge, or amount with raw `Color()` literals — `feedback.*` tokens are the source of truth.
Use this for non-transactional rows (notifications, settings, search) — pick a neutral list-item primitive instead.

ios.kdoc:
A status-tinted transaction row for payment / withdrawal / savings
history lists. The SwiftUI port of aurum-android's
`AurumTransactionListCard` (Figma node `5576:19749`).

Outer `feedback.*` wash wraps a white inner card with leading slot,
label + date, status badge, and amount (+ optional gold-weight suffix).
`helperText` renders below in the matching status colour.

components.name: AurumChip
components.summary: Aurum's chip — a pill-shaped tappable element.
components.kdoc:
Aurum's chip — a pill-shaped tappable element. Renders Figma's chip
component set at file YvIxqu2oTlM6UnkL0OloHc, node 4446-701, across both
`Action` and `Other` types. Slot composition picks the Figma chip role
within `Other`:

  - label only — Suggestion
  - `leadingIcon` + label — Assist
  - `leadingIcon` + label + `trailingIcon` — Filter / Input

`Action` is the canonical label + trailing chevron CTA chip and carries
the `Brand` / `Black` colour variants.
components.signature:
@Composable
fun AurumChip(
    label: String,
    onClick: () -> Unit,
    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
    type: AurumChipType = AurumChipType.Other,
    variant: AurumChipVariant = AurumChipVariant.Neutral,
    size: AurumChipSize = AurumChipSize.Large,
    enabled: Boolean = true,
    leadingIcon: ImageVector? = null,
    trailingIcon: ImageVector? = null,
)
components.intendedUse: Single tappable category/filter selector or a brand action surface in dense layouts. Short labels (1–2 words). Prefer [com.jar.app.aurum.component.button.AurumButton] for primary actions and [com.jar.app.aurum.component.selection.AurumRadioGroup] for mutually- exclusive choices.

do: Pair `leadingIcon` with monosemic glyphs (filter, sort, history).
Use `Small` in dense rows; `Large` for one-off CTAs in section headers.
Reserve `Brand` for the primary CTA on a surface (≤1 per screen).
Stretch with `Modifier.weight(1f)` from a `RowScope` for equally-sized chips across a row.
dont: Use chips as form input — switch to `AurumRadioGroup` for exclusive choices.
Override colours via `Modifier.background` / `tint`; recolour through `variant` only.
Stack vertically — chips flow horizontally with `Arrangement.spacedBy(s2)`.

components.name: AurumPill
components.summary: Aurum's pill — a compact, label-first status / token element.
components.signature:
@Composable
fun AurumPill(
    label: String,
    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
    type: AurumPillType = AurumPillType.Rounded,
    selected: Boolean = false,
    enabled: Boolean = true,
    leadingIcon: ImageVector? = null,
    trailingIcon: ImageVector? = null,
    tag: String? = null,
    onClick: (() -> Unit)? = null,
)

params.name: label
params.doc: the pill text.

params.name: modifier
params.doc: applied to the pill root.

params.name: type
params.doc: the Figma `type` axis — see [AurumPillType].

params.name: selected
params.doc: the active look (Figma `state=focus`): primary border + bold label.

params.name: enabled
params.doc: when false renders the Figma `state=disabled` look and drops the click.

params.name: leadingIcon
params.doc: optional 16dp glyph before the label (Rectangular / Rounded only).

params.name: trailingIcon
params.doc: optional 16dp glyph after the label (Rectangular / Rounded only).

params.name: tag
params.doc: the uppercase ribbon text for [AurumPillType.WithTags]; ignored otherwise.

params.name: onClick
params.doc: optional — when set, the pill is clickable (role = Button).

components.intendedUse: A small label-first token for status, category, or a selectable filter where a full [AurumChip] would shout. Short labels (1–2 words). For primary actions use [com.jar.app.aurum.component.button.AurumButton]; for an action / filter CTA use [AurumChip].

do: Use [AurumPillType.Rounded] by default; reserve [AurumPillType.Rectangular] for grid/table cells where square-ish corners read better.
Drive the active look with `selected` (a selectable pill), not by recolouring.
Pair `leadingIcon` with a monosemic glyph (filter, location, tag).
Use [AurumPillType.WithTags] with `tag` for a pill that carries a small category/discount ribbon.
dont: Recolour via `Modifier.background` / `tint` — the state set is fixed; use `selected` / `enabled`.
Use a pill as a primary action — switch to [com.jar.app.aurum.component.button.AurumButton].
Pass both icons on [AurumPillType.WithTags] — that type is label + tag only.

components.name: AurumDivider
components.summary: Aurum's divider primitive — a thin horizontal line used to separate content sections.

params.name: modifier
params.doc: the [Modifier] to apply

params.name: color
params.doc: the divider colour — defaults to the surface low-contrast

params.name: thickness
params.doc: the divider height — defaults to [Aurum.borderWidth.xs]

components.intendedUse: Use as a thin separator between rows in a list, or before/after a logical content group. Keeps the surface scannable without introducing a new section affordance. For section headings use [AurumSectionHeader]; for layout spacing use `Aurum.space.*` tokens (a Spacer with the right height is structurally cheaper than a divider).

do: Use the default `borderSurfaceLowContrast` colour — it's tuned to be visible without competing with content.
Reach for `borderWidth.xs` (default, hairline) — thicker dividers read as decoration rather than separation.
dont: Use as decorative page-break flourish — that's a typography or layout concern, not a divider.
Use for layout spacing — a `Spacer(Modifier.height(Aurum.space.s5))` is the right tool when you just need air, not a visible line.

params.name: modifier
params.doc: the [Modifier] to apply

params.name: color
params.doc: the divider colour — defaults to the surface low-contrast

params.name: thickness
params.doc: the divider height — defaults to [Aurum.borderWidth.xs]

ios.kdoc:
Aurum's divider primitive — a thin horizontal line used to separate
content sections. The SwiftUI port of aurum-android's `AurumDivider`:
a styled rectangle, not `SwiftUI.Divider` (whose hairline colour and
thickness are system-controlled, not token-controlled).

components.name: AurumBadge
components.summary: Aurum's badge primitive — a small, non-interactive label used for status, categorization, or count indicators.
components.kdoc:
Aurum's badge primitive — a small, non-interactive label used for
status, categorization, or count indicators.

Supports 6 colour roles × 2 emphases × 2 sizes, with an optional
leading icon. Tokens are sourced from the `feedback.*` family in
[Aurum.colors].

This is a **scratch** component — built from [Box] + [Row], no
`material3` imports.

## Token pairing (cross-axis)

Emphasis and text/icon colours follow a **cross-axis** pairing:
- `Intense` background → `Inverse` text/icon (e.g., solid purple bg +
  white text)
- `Subtle` background → `Intense` text/icon (e.g., light wash bg +
  saturated purple text)

This is by design and matches the Figma token references.

## Figma spec

- Height: 24 dp (default), 28 dp (large)
- Horizontal padding: `spacing[4]` = 12 dp
- Icon-to-label gap: `spacing[2]` = 4 dp
- Shape: pill (`border.radius.max`)
- Text: `Label/L-Medium` (12 sp Inter, 500 Medium, 16 sp line-height)
- Icon size: `iconSize.md` = 16 dp (fits within badge height)

@param label the badge text
@param modifier the [Modifier] to apply
@param color the colour role — defaults to [AurumBadgeColor.Primary]
@param emphasis the visual intensity — defaults to [AurumBadgeEmphasis.Intense]
@param size the badge size — defaults to [AurumBadgeSize.Default]
@param leadingIcon optional icon drawn before the label; sized to
  [Aurum.iconSize.md] (16 dp)
components.signature:
@Composable
fun AurumBadge(
    label: String,
    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
    color: AurumBadgeColor = AurumBadgeColor.Primary,
    emphasis: AurumBadgeEmphasis = AurumBadgeEmphasis.Intense,
    size: AurumBadgeSize = AurumBadgeSize.Default,
    leadingIcon: ImageVector? = null,
)

params.doc: the badge text

params.name: modifier

params.name: emphasis
params.doc: the visual intensity — defaults to [AurumBadgeEmphasis.Intense]

params.name: size
params.doc: the badge size — defaults to [AurumBadgeSize.Default]

params.name: leadingIcon
params.doc: optional icon drawn before the label; sized to

components.intendedUse: Use as a small status / categorisation / count indicator anchored to another piece of content — "New" pill on a list item, "Verified" stamp on a profile, unread count on an avatar. The `Intense` emphasis pulls the eye; `Subtle` is the same role at lower volume. For longer-running ephemeral feedback use [AurumToast]; for tappable filters use [AurumChip].

do: Pick the `color` from semantic intent — Positive for success, Negative for failure, Notice for warnings, Info for neutral context.
Anchor the badge to its subject (top-right of an icon, inline before a label) so the relationship is unambiguous.
Keep labels to 1–2 words; the pill clamps at `iconSize.md` height and long text wraps awkwardly.
dont: Use as a tappable affordance — badges are non-interactive. For tap actions use [AurumChip] (filter / select) or [AurumIconButton] (action).
Stack badges vertically on a single subject; one anchored badge per item conveys status best.
Mix multiple `color` roles on the same surface for the same kind of subject — readers infer hierarchy from the role choice.

)

This is a **scratch** component — built from [Box] + [Row], no
`material3` imports.

## Token pairing (cross-axis)

Emphasis and text/icon colours follow a **cross-axis** pairing:
- `Intense` background → `Inverse` text/icon (e.g., solid purple bg +
  white text)
- `Subtle` background → `Intense` text/icon (e.g., light wash bg +
  saturated purple text)

This is by design and matches the Figma token references.

## Figma spec

- Height: 24 dp (default), 28 dp (large)
- Horizontal padding: `spacing[4]` = 12 dp
- Icon-to-label gap: `spacing[2]` = 4 dp
- Shape: pill (`border.radius.max`)
- Text: `Label/L-Medium` (12 sp Inter, 500 Medium, 16 sp line-height)
- Icon size: `iconSize.md` = 16 dp (fits within badge height)

@param label the badge text
@param modifier the [Modifier] to apply
@param color the colour role — defaults to [AurumBadgeColor.Primary]
@param emphasis the visual intensity — defaults to [AurumBadgeEmphasis.Intense]
@param size the badge size — defaults to [AurumBadgeSize.Default]
@param leadingIcon optional icon drawn before the label; sized to
  [Aurum.iconSize.md] (16 dp)

ios.kdoc:
Aurum's badge primitive — a small, non-interactive label used for
status, categorization, or count indicators. The SwiftUI port of
aurum-android's `AurumBadge`.

Supports 6 colour roles × 2 emphases × 2 sizes, with an optional
leading icon. Emphasis and text/icon colours follow a **cross-axis**
pairing by design (matching the Figma token references): `intense`
background → `inverse` content; `subtle` background → `intense` content.

components.name: AurumBanner
components.summary: Aurum's banner primitive — a persistent, top-anchored, full-bleed tonal status strip.
components.kdoc:
Aurum's banner primitive — a persistent, top-anchored, full-bleed tonal
status strip. It is the **persistent** counterpart of the transient
[AurumToast]: same tonal palette and status glyphs, but structural
(square corners, full-bleed, a bottom hairline) rather than a floating
rounded pill, and it stays until the caller dismisses it.

Each [tone] pairs a `feedback.*` subtle background with the matching
intense content colour and a per-tone leading status glyph. An opaque
`surface.bgPageBase` base is painted under the tint so the strip reads
solid over any backdrop. Optionally renders a single inline text
[action] (e.g. "Retry") and, when [onDismiss] is non-null, a trailing
close ✕.

This is a **presentational** primitive — it does not own the show/hide
lifecycle. Pair it with caller-owned state (or [AurumBannerHost]).

@param message the status message rendered inside the strip
@param modifier the [Modifier] to apply
@param tone semantic colour role — defaults to [AurumBannerTone.Neutral]
@param leadingIcon optional glyph override — defaults (`null`) to the
  tone's status glyph. Supply to swap the glyph; it is still tinted to
  the tone's content colour and sized by the banner.
@param action optional inline text action (e.g. "Retry") rendered at the
  trailing edge — defaults (`null`) to no action
@param windowInsets insets padded *inside* the tone background (M3
  app-bar convention) — pass `WindowInsets.statusBars` when the band is
  anchored to the top window edge so the tone colour paints under the
  status bar while content stays clear of it. Defaults to none.
@param onDismiss optional dismiss callback — `null` (default) makes the
  banner non-dismissible (state banners); non-null renders a trailing ✕

components.intendedUse: Use for persistent, top-anchored, app- or page-level status the user must stay aware of — offline / no-connection, scheduled downtime, degraded service, account warnings (KYC pending, payment failed). The persistent counterpart to the transient [AurumToast]: pick the [tone] from semantic intent. For ephemeral confirmations use [AurumToast]; for modal / destructive flows use [com.jar.app.aurum.component.sheet.AurumBottomSheet].

do: Use for persistent state the user must stay aware of, and pick [tone] from semantic intent — Error for failures, Warning for at-risk state, Info for neutral context, Success for resolved state, Brand for product moments, Neutral for low-urgency system status.
Pair a single [action] only when there is a clear recovery path (e.g. "Retry", "Verify now"); leave it `null` for pure status.
Set [onDismiss] for advisory banners the user can clear; leave it `null` for state banners that must persist until the underlying condition resolves.
dont: Use for transient confirmations like "Saved" or "Copied" — that's [AurumToast].
Stack multiple banners — show one at a time so the top-anchored slot stays a single, clear signal.
Clip the strip or give it rounded corners — the banner is a structural full-bleed band, not a floating card.

ios.kdoc:
Aurum's banner primitive — a persistent, top-anchored, full-bleed tonal
status strip. The SwiftUI port of aurum-android's `AurumBanner`. It is
the **persistent** counterpart of the transient `AurumToast`: same tonal
palette and status glyphs, but structural (square corners, full-bleed, a
bottom hairline) rather than a floating rounded pill, and it stays until
the caller dismisses it.

Each `tone` pairs a `feedback.*` subtle background with the matching
intense content colour and a per-tone leading status glyph. An opaque
`bgPageBase` base is painted under the tint so the strip reads solid
over any backdrop. Optionally renders a single inline text `action`
(e.g. "Retry") and, when `onDismiss` is non-nil, a trailing close ✕.

When the banner touches the top screen edge its backgrounds bleed under
the status bar automatically (SwiftUI `background` extends into safe
areas) while the content stays clear — the equivalent of Android's
`windowInsets` parameter, with no parameter needed.

This is a **presentational** primitive — it does not own the show/hide
lifecycle. Pair it with caller-owned state (or `AurumBannerHost`).

components.name: AurumBannerHost
components.summary: Aurum's banner host — a thin slot that renders the single currently visible [AurumBanner] from [hostState] with a slide-from-top expand/collapse animation.
components.kdoc:
Aurum's banner host — a thin slot that renders the single currently
visible [AurumBanner] from [hostState] with a slide-from-top
expand/collapse animation.

Place at the **top** of your Compose tree (above the app bar / content).
Placed **in the layout flow** (first child of the root Column, or a
top-anchored slot the rest of the chrome is constrained below), the
expand/shrink animation smoothly pushes the content underneath down and
releases it back — the structural-band behaviour. Placed in an overlay
Box it floats over content instead. Hoist [AurumBannerHostState] with [rememberAurumBannerHostState]
and drive it via `hostState.show(...)` / `hostState.dismiss()`. Banners
are persistent — there is no queue or timer; the caller owns the
lifecycle.

This is **codebase-only** — there is **no Figma node yet**. It is
proposal-grade orchestration glue, pending design; reconcile when design
produces the spec. The visual is [AurumBanner].

@param hostState state holder (`rememberAurumBannerHostState()`)
@param modifier the [Modifier] to apply to the animation wrapper
@param banner renderer slot. Defaults to the canonical [AurumBanner]
  with [Modifier.fillMaxWidth]. Override to inject custom placement.
components.signature:
@Composable
fun AurumBannerHost(
    hostState: AurumBannerHostState,
    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
    banner: @Composable (AurumBannerData) -> Unit = { DefaultBannerRender(hostState,
    it) },
)

params.name: hostState
params.doc: state holder (`rememberAurumBannerHostState()`)

params.name: modifier
params.doc: the [Modifier] to apply to the animation wrapper

params.name: banner
params.doc: renderer slot. Defaults to the canonical [AurumBanner]

components.intendedUse: Host slot for persistent, top-anchored, app- or page-level status: offline / no-connection, scheduled downtime, degraded service, account warnings. One host per top-level Compose tree (the screen / nav-graph host), placed top-anchored. The caller supplies the status-bar inset via the modifier (e.g. `Modifier.statusBarsPadding()`).

do: Place the host top-anchored, above the app bar / content, so the strip reads as a structural band at the top edge.
Supply the status-bar inset yourself via the modifier (e.g. `Modifier.statusBarsPadding()`) — the host doesn't pad insets itself.
Drive `show(...)` / `dismiss()` from the source of truth for the condition (e.g. a connectivity flow), so the banner mirrors live state.
dont: Use this for transient confirmations — that's [AurumToastHost] + `showToast(...)`.
Mount more than one [AurumBannerHost] per screen — concurrent hosts fight for the top slot.

Hand the host its own `Modifier.fillMaxSize()` — it occupies only the banner's natural height. Wrap in `Box(Modifier.fillMaxSize())` and align it via `Modifier.align(Alignment.TopCenter)` for top-edge anchoring.

ios.kdoc:
Aurum's banner host — a thin slot that renders the single currently
visible `AurumBanner` from `hostState` with a slide-from-top
expand/collapse animation.

Place at the **top** of the screen (above the header / content). Placed
**in the layout flow** (first child of the root `VStack`), the animation
smoothly pushes the content underneath down and releases it back — the
structural-band behaviour. Placed in an `.overlay(alignment: .top)` it
floats over content instead. Banners are persistent — there is no queue
or timer; the caller owns the lifecycle.

This is **codebase-only** — there is **no Figma node yet**. It is
proposal-grade orchestration glue, pending design; reconcile when design
produces the spec. The visual is `AurumBanner`.

components.name: AurumLoader
components.summary: Aurum's circular progress spinner.
components.signature:
@Composable
fun AurumLoader(
    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
    color: Color = Aurum.colors.interactive.bgPrimaryDefault,
    trackColor: Color = Aurum.colors.surface.bgSurfaceMidContrast,
    strokeWidth: Dp = Aurum.borderWidth.lg,
    size: Dp = Aurum.iconSize.xxl,
    label: String? = null,
    labelPosition: AurumLoaderLabelPosition = AurumLoaderLabelPosition.Right,
)

params.name: modifier
params.doc: the [Modifier] to apply to the root (the spinner, or the spinner+label row/column).

params.name: color
params.doc: the spinning arc colour — defaults to the primary interactive background token.

params.name: trackColor
params.doc: the background ring colour — defaults to the mid-contrast surface token.

params.name: strokeWidth
params.doc: the thickness of the ring — defaults to [Aurum.borderWidth.lg].

params.name: size
params.doc: the overall diameter — defaults to [Aurum.iconSize.xxl].

params.name: label
params.doc: optional text shown alongside the spinner (Figma `shownLabel`); null = spinner only.

params.name: labelPosition
params.doc: where the [label] sits relative to the spinner — see [AurumLoaderLabelPosition].

components.intendedUse: Use for short-duration, action-bound waits where the user has just initiated something — submitting a form, fetching a fresh result, retrying after an error. Rule of thumb: if the wait could exceed ~2 seconds and you have content shape to suggest, use [AurumSkeleton] instead so the surface keeps its perceived layout.

do: Render inline next to the action that triggered the wait (e.g. inside [com.jar.app.aurum.component.button.AurumButton]'s `isLoading` slot, an inline list-row spinner).
Pass a `label` ("Loading…") for screen-level waits so the affordance reads clearly; the label also gives screen readers something to announce.
Default `size` to [Aurum.iconSize.xxl] for screen-level waits; use [Aurum.iconSize.md] for inline-button spinners.
dont: Use for long page loads — multi-second blank screens are perceived as broken; switch to [AurumSkeleton].
Stack multiple spinners on the same screen for parallel requests; aggregate to one canonical "loading" affordance.

ios.kdoc:
Aurum's circular progress spinner — the SwiftUI port of aurum-android's
`AurumLoader`. Renders Figma's "Spinner" set (file `YvIxqu2oTlM6UnkL0OloHc`,
node `4530-2084`): a full-circle track in `trackColor` with a 90-degree arc
in `color` rotating at constant speed, an optional `label` (Figma
`shownLabel`) placed `.right` or `.bottom`, and free `size` / `color`
(the Figma `size` and `color` axes are covered by these continuous params).

components.name: AurumProgressBar
components.summary: Aurum's linear progress bar — a determinate 8dp track with a primary-fill indicator.

    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,

    showPercentage: Boolean = false,

params.name: showPercentage
params.doc: when true, shows the rounded percentage in the header (trailing).

components.intendedUse: A determinate progress indicator for a known, bounded task — onboarding steps, upload/download, a multi-stage flow. Drive [progress] from your state (0f..1f). For unknown-duration waits use [AurumLoader]; for stepped/story progress use `AurumSegmentedProgressBar`.

do: Pass a `0f..1f` fraction; values are coerced into range.
Animate at the call site (`animateFloatAsState`) when progress jumps, so the fill glides.
Use `label` + `showPercentage` to give the bar context in a header row.
dont: Use it for indeterminate / unknown-duration work — that's [AurumLoader].
Recolour the track/indicator — the tokens are fixed (neutral track, primary fill).

primary-fill indicator. The SwiftUI port of aurum-android's
`AurumProgressBar`: an 8 pt pill track (`interactive.bgNeutralDefault`)
with a primary indicator sized to `progress`, plus an optional header
row (leading `label`, trailing percentage).

Determinate only — for an indeterminate spinner use `AurumLoader`.

components.name: AurumSegmentedProgressBar
components.summary: Aurum's segmented progress bar — discrete progress across [total] segments, the first [filled] of them complete.
components.signature:
@Composable
fun AurumSegmentedProgressBar(
    filled: Int,
    total: Int,
    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
    type: AurumSegmentedProgressType = AurumSegmentedProgressType.Bars,
    intent: AurumSegmentIntent = AurumSegmentIntent.Default,
    label: String? = null,
    showPercentage: Boolean = false,
)

params.name: filled
params.doc: the number of completed segments, coerced to `0..total`.

params.name: total
params.doc: the segment count (coerced to at least 1).

params.name: modifier
params.doc: applied to the root column.

params.name: type
params.doc: bars or numbered bubbles — see [AurumSegmentedProgressType].

params.name: intent
params.doc: the filled colour family — see [AurumSegmentIntent].

params.name: label
params.doc: optional header text shown above the bar (leading).

params.name: showPercentage
params.doc: when true, shows the rounded `filled/total` percentage (trailing).

components.intendedUse: Discrete, countable progress — onboarding steps, a multi-step form, a quiz, a story/reel position. Use [AurumSegmentedProgressType.Bubbles] when each step is a meaningful, numbered checkpoint; [AurumSegmentedProgressType.Bars] when it's just N of M. For a continuous fraction use [AurumProgressBar]; for indeterminate waits use [AurumLoader].

do: Pass `filled` / `total` as counts (e.g. 3 of 5); both are coerced into range.
Use `intent` to carry status — e.g. [AurumSegmentIntent.Negative] for a failed step run.
Use `Bubbles` only for ≤ ~5 steps; beyond that the numerals get cramped — prefer `Bars`.
dont: Use it for a continuous 0–100% value — that's [AurumProgressBar].
Recolour segments directly — pick the fixed `intent` palette instead.

ios.kdoc:
Aurum's segmented progress bar — discrete progress across `total`
segments, the first `filled` of them complete. The SwiftUI port of
aurum-android's `AurumSegmentedProgressBar`:

- `.bars` — `total` equal 8 pt pill segments; the first `filled` are
tinted by `intent`, the rest neutral.
- `.bubbles` — the same, each segment capped by a 24 pt numbered
checkpoint; completed steps fill + carry inverse-tinted numerals.

components.name: AurumSkeleton
components.summary: Aurum's shimmer/skeleton loading placeholder.
components.kdoc:
Aurum's shimmer/skeleton loading placeholder.

A **scratch** component — built on [Box] from `androidx.compose.foundation`,
not derived from any Material 3 widget. Replaces the 5+ per-feature shimmer
implementations scattered across the codebase.

The skeleton renders a solid mid-contrast background with a translucent
shimmer gradient that animates horizontally from left to right. Callers
control the shape via the [shape] parameter and dimensions via [modifier].

Typical usage:
```kotlin
// Text-line placeholder
AurumSkeleton(Modifier.fillMaxWidth().height(Aurum.space.s5))

// Avatar placeholder
AurumSkeleton(
    modifier = Modifier.size(Aurum.space.s9),
    shape = CircleShape,
)
```

@param modifier the [Modifier] to apply — set width/height here to define
  the placeholder dimensions
@param shape the clipping shape — defaults to [Aurum.shape.sm]

params.name: modifier
params.doc: the [Modifier] to apply — set width/height here to define

params.name: shape
params.doc: the clipping shape — defaults to [Aurum.shape.sm]

components.intendedUse: Use as a content-shaped placeholder during the initial fetch on list / detail surfaces, where the eventual layout is known and the wait is multi-second. Keeps the surface visually stable so users perceive less latency. For action-bound short waits use [AurumLoader] instead.

do: Match the skeleton dimensions and shape to the eventual content (avatar circle, full-width text-line height) so the swap-in is invisible.
Render multiple skeletons with realistic variation in widths to mimic real text — uniform-width rows look fake.
Keep the shimmer running until real content is ready; never freeze on a static skeleton frame.
dont: Use for short single-action waits — that's [AurumLoader].
Render skeletons indefinitely on error states — switch to a meaningful empty/error view after a reasonable timeout.
Replace text-line skeletons with bare horizontal lines — pad to the realistic line height so the layout doesn't jump on swap-in.

params.name: modifier
params.doc: the [Modifier] to apply — set width/height here to define

params.name: shape
params.doc: the clipping shape — defaults to [Aurum.shape.sm]

Aurum's shimmer/skeleton loading placeholder — the SwiftUI port of
aurum-android's `AurumSkeleton`.

Renders a solid mid-contrast background with a translucent shimmer
gradient that animates horizontally left to right. Callers control the
clipping shape via the initializer and dimensions via `.frame(...)`:

// Text-line placeholder
AurumSkeleton()
.frame(height: Aurum.space.s5)

// Avatar placeholder
AurumSkeleton(shape: Circle())
.frame(width: Aurum.space.s9, height: Aurum.space.s9)

components.name: AurumToast
components.summary: Aurum's toast primitive — a non-blocking, dismissible status strip surfaced over content.
components.kdoc:
Aurum's toast primitive — a non-blocking, dismissible status strip
surfaced over content. Renders Figma's toast component set at file
YvIxqu2oTlM6UnkL0OloHc, node 6014:2621.

Six [tone] roles × two [size]s = 12 visual variants. Each tone pairs
a `feedback.*` subtle background with the matching intense
text/icon, plus a per-tone leading status glyph from
[AurumIcons.Status] / [AurumIcons.Selection]. A trailing close
[AurumIconButton] is always present and fires [onDismiss].

This is a **presentational** primitive — it does not own the
show/hide lifecycle, queue, or auto-dismiss timer. Pair it with
caller-owned state for visibility (or with a future
`rememberToastHostState`-style controller).

@param label the status message rendered inside the strip
@param onDismiss called when the trailing close icon is tapped
@param modifier the [Modifier] to apply
@param tone semantic colour role — defaults to [AurumToastTone.Neutral]
@param size visual size — defaults to [AurumToastSize.Small]
@param showLeadingIcon whether to render the per-tone leading status
  glyph — defaults to `true`. Set `false` when the surrounding
  context already conveys tone (e.g. inside a coloured banner).
@param leadingIcon optional glyph override — defaults (`null`) to the
  tone's status icon. Supply to swap the glyph; it is still tinted to the
  tone's content colour and sized by the toast. Ignored when
  [showLeadingIcon] is `false`.
components.signature:
@Composable
fun AurumToast(
    label: String,
    onDismiss: () -> Unit,
    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
    tone: AurumToastTone = AurumToastTone.Neutral,
    size: AurumToastSize = AurumToastSize.Small,
    showLeadingIcon: Boolean = true,
    leadingIcon: ImageVector? = null,
)

params.doc: the status message rendered inside the strip

params.name: onDismiss

params.doc: semantic colour role — defaults to [AurumToastTone.Neutral]

params.name: size
params.doc: visual size — defaults to [AurumToastSize.Small]

params.name: showLeadingIcon
params.doc: whether to render the per-tone leading status

params.name: leadingIcon
params.doc: optional glyph override — defaults (`null`) to the

components.intendedUse: Use to surface lightweight, asynchronous feedback tied to a recent user action — "Saved", "Copied", "Couldn't fetch, retrying", "New version available". Pick the [tone] from semantic intent: Positive for success, Negative for error, Notice for warnings, Info for neutral context, Brand for product moments, Neutral for system feedback without urgency. For modal confirmations or destructive flows use [com.jar.app.aurum.component.sheet.AurumBottomSheet]; for inline form validation use the input components' built-in error affordances.

do: Pick [tone] from semantic intent — never style a Neutral toast red to "look like an error", choose [AurumToastTone.Error] instead.
Use [AurumToastSize.Small] for confirmations that fit in 1–4 words; reach for [AurumToastSize.Large] only when the message genuinely needs to wrap.
Pair the toast with a caller-owned visibility state and auto-dismiss timer so it disappears without forcing the user to tap the X.
dont: Stack two toasts of different tones — show the newer one and let it replace the prior.

Embed buttons / link affordances inside the [label] — the toast is a status strip, not an action surface. If you need an action, prefer [com.jar.app.aurum.component.sheet.AurumBottomSheet] or a contextual CTA next to the originating control.
Use a toast as the only feedback channel for irreversible actions — surface those through a confirmation sheet so the user has a chance to react before the strip fades.

ios.kdoc:
Aurum's toast primitive — a non-blocking, dismissible status strip
surfaced over content. The SwiftUI port of aurum-android's `AurumToast`;
renders Figma's toast component set (node 6014:2621).

Six `tone` roles × two `size`s = 12 visual variants. Each tone pairs a
`feedback.*` subtle background with the matching intense text/icon, plus
a per-tone leading status glyph. An opaque `bgPageBase` base is painted
under the tint so the strip reads solid over any backdrop. A trailing
close `AurumIconButton` is always present and fires `onDismiss`.

This is a **presentational** primitive — it does not own the show/hide
lifecycle, queue, or auto-dismiss timer. Pair it with caller-owned state
for visibility, or with `AurumToastHost` + `AurumToastHostState`.

components.name: AurumToastHost
components.summary: Aurum's toast host — a thin overlay slot that renders the single currently visible [AurumToast] from [hostState] with enter/exit animation.
components.kdoc:
Aurum's toast host — a thin overlay slot that renders the single
currently visible [AurumToast] from [hostState] with enter/exit
animation. Drives auto-dismiss via [AurumToastDuration].

Place at the bottom of your Compose tree, usually inside a Compose
Scaffold's overlay region or alongside the bottom system inset.
Hoist [AurumToastHostState] alongside a `rememberCoroutineScope()`
and trigger via `scope.launch { hostState.showToast("Saved") }`.

This is **codebase-only** — there is no Figma equivalent. It is
orchestration glue, not a visual primitive. The visual is
[AurumToast].

@param hostState state holder (`rememberAurumToastHostState()`)
@param modifier the [Modifier] to apply to the animation wrapper
@param toast renderer slot. Defaults to the canonical [AurumToast]
  with [Modifier.fillMaxWidth] and 16 dp horizontal padding. Override
  to inject custom width / placement.

params.name: hostState
params.doc: state holder (`rememberAurumToastHostState()`)

params.name: modifier
params.doc: the [Modifier] to apply to the animation wrapper

params.name: toast
params.doc: renderer slot. Defaults to the canonical [AurumToast]

components.intendedUse: Host slot for all in-app, programmatic notifications: confirmations, validation feedback, sync results, retry prompts. One host per top-level Compose tree (the screen / nav graph host). Replaces system `Toast.makeText` and ad-hoc Compose snackbars in feature code.

do: Pair with a `rememberCoroutineScope()` so feature code can `scope.launch { hostState.showToast(...) }` from click handlers / flow collectors.
Place the host inside the same `Box`/Scaffold that owns the screen's bottom system-insets — the host doesn't pad insets itself.
Use [AurumToastDuration.Short] for confirmations, [Long] for messages that need reading, [Indefinite] for actions awaiting an explicit dismiss.
dont: Render an [AurumToast] outside a host when the message is programmatic / asynchronous — without the queue + timer you'll either drop messages or stack them.
Mount more than one [AurumToastHost] per screen — concurrent hosts fight for the bottom slot and produce racy overlays.

Hand the host its own `Modifier.fillMaxSize()` — it occupies only the toast's natural height. Wrap in `Box(Modifier.fillMaxSize())` and align it via `Modifier.align(Alignment.BottomCenter)` if you want bottom-edge anchoring.

params.name: modifier
params.doc: the [Modifier] to apply to the animation wrapper

params.name: toast
params.doc: renderer slot. Defaults to the canonical [AurumToast]

and trigger via `scope.launch { hostState.showToast("Saved") }`.

This is **codebase-only** — there is no Figma equivalent. It is

[AurumToast].

@param hostState state holder (`rememberAurumToastHostState()`)
@param modifier the [Modifier] to apply to the animation wrapper
@param toast renderer slot. Defaults to the canonical [AurumToast]

ios.kdoc:
Aurum's toast host — a thin overlay slot that renders the single
currently visible `AurumToast` from `hostState` with slide-up/fade
enter/exit animation, and drives auto-dismiss via `AurumToastDuration`.

Place at the bottom of the screen, typically bottom-aligned in an
overlay: `.overlay(alignment: .bottom) { AurumToastHost(hostState:
toastHost) }`. Hoist `AurumToastHostState` with `@State` and trigger via
`Task { await hostState.showToast("Saved") }`.

This is **codebase-only** — there is no Figma equivalent. It is
orchestration glue, not a visual primitive. The visual is `AurumToast`.

components.name: AurumAmountInput
components.summary: Aurum's currency-prefixed numeric input.
components.signature:
@Composable
fun AurumAmountInput(
    value: String,
    onValueChange: (String) -> Unit,
    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
    label: String? = null,
    helperText: String? = null,
    errorText: String? = null,
    successText: String? = null,
    currencySymbol: String = "₹",
    suffixText: String? = null,
    enabled: Boolean = true,
    readOnly: Boolean = false,
    loading: Boolean = false,
)

params.name: value
params.doc: the current text value — already formatted for display

params.name: onValueChange
params.doc: called with the raw string the user typed (no automatic formatting)

params.name: modifier
params.doc: applied to the outer column

params.name: label
params.doc: optional label displayed above the field

params.name: helperText
params.doc: optional assistive text shown below the field

params.name: errorText
params.doc: when non-null, the field shows error styling and this replaces [helperText]

params.name: successText
params.doc: when non-null (and no error), the field shows success styling

params.name: currencySymbol
params.doc: the leading currency glyph — defaults to `"₹"`

params.name: suffixText
params.doc: optional trailing equivalent; hidden while [loading]

params.name: enabled
params.doc: whether the field accepts input

params.name: readOnly
params.doc: focusable but not editable; also forced read-only while [loading]

params.name: loading
params.doc: when `true`, the field is read-only and a small inline loader replaces [suffixText]

components.intendedUse: Use whenever the user is entering a monetary value — buy-gold amount, top-up, transfer, withdrawal target. The currency prefix and numeric keyboard make money-vs-text intent unambiguous.

do: Pre-format the displayed value in the caller (`"99,999"`, `"1,00,000"`) and pass the formatted string straight to [value].
Use [suffixText] for a derived equivalent (gold grams, USD equivalent) — keep the leading slot for the *primary* currency.
Set [loading] while the equivalent value (e.g. live gold rate) is being fetched — this both blocks edits and signals progress.
dont: Pass an unformatted long like `"99999"` if the design shows a grouped amount — format upstream with the user's locale.
Use this for non-monetary numeric input — reach for [AurumTextField] with [KeyboardType.Number] instead.
Render two amount inputs side-by-side competing for focus — monetary entry should be the hero of its surface.

ios.kdoc:
An amount-entry field: currency symbol + hero numeric value + optional
derived suffix (e.g. gold grams). The SwiftUI port of aurum-android's
`AurumAmountInput`.

Delegates to `AurumTextField` with the entered amount as the hero of the
field — Num/XL per Figma vs the generic field's body style, with the
currency symbol one step down at Num/L.

components.name: AurumOtpInput
components.summary: Aurum's OTP (one-time password) input component.
components.signature:
@Composable
fun AurumOtpInput(
    value: String,
    onValueChange: (String) -> Unit,
    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
    length: AurumOtpLength = AurumOtpLength.Six,
    label: String? = null,
    helperText: String? = null,
    validationState: AurumOtpValidationState = AurumOtpValidationState.None,
    enabled: Boolean = true,
    onComplete: ((String) -> Unit)? = null,
)

params.name: value
params.doc: the current OTP string (digits only, length <= [length].count)

params.name: onValueChange
params.doc: callback invoked when the user types or deletes a digit

params.name: modifier
params.doc: the [Modifier] to apply to the outer column

params.name: length
params.doc: the number of digit cells — 4 or 6

params.name: label
params.doc: optional label text displayed above the cells

params.name: helperText
params.doc: optional helper/error/success text displayed below the cells

params.name: validationState
params.doc: controls underline and helper text colour

params.name: enabled
params.doc: whether the input accepts user interaction

params.name: onComplete
params.doc: optional callback invoked once all digits have been entered

components.intendedUse: Use whenever the user must enter a numeric one-time password — login OTP, payment authorisation, transaction confirmation. The cell count ([Length.Four] or [Length.Six]) should match the OTP length your backend issues. Don't compose digit cells from `AurumTextField` — this component already handles paste, autofill, and resend-timer UX correctly.

do: Use `validationState = Error` together with a meaningful `helperText` to guide retries — never silently mark the OTP wrong.
Track resend countdown with `validationState = Timer` so the helper colour reflects the wait state without changing layout.
Hook `onComplete` to auto-submit when all cells are filled — saves the user a tap.
dont: Pass non-digit characters via `onValueChange`; the cells render but the underlying keyboard is numeric and behaviour is undefined.
Render multiple OTP inputs on one screen — there's only ever one active OTP context.
Use this for non-numeric verification (e.g. invitation codes); use `AurumTextField` instead.

ios.kdoc:
Aurum's OTP (one-time password) input — the SwiftUI port of
aurum-android's `AurumOtpInput`.

Renders a row of underline-style digit cells that fill sequentially. A
single invisible `TextField` captures keyboard input (numeric keypad,
`oneTimeCode` content type so SMS autofill works); the visible cells are
decorative columns (digit + coloured underline bar). The active cell's
brand underline is the sole caret cue.

components.name: AurumPhoneNumberInput
components.summary: A phone-number-specific text field: country code + divider + digit input.
components.signature:
@Composable
fun AurumPhoneNumberInput(
    value: String,
    onValueChange: (String) -> Unit,
    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
    countryCode: String = "+91",
    flag: (@Composable () -> Unit)? = null,
    label: String? = null,
    placeholder: String = "Phone Number",
    helperText: String? = null,
    errorText: String? = null,
    successText: String? = null,
    isError: Boolean = errorText != null,
    isSuccess: Boolean = successText != null && !isError,
    isLoading: Boolean = false,
    enabled: Boolean = true,
    readOnly: Boolean = false,
    maxLength: Int = 10,
    keyboardActions: KeyboardActions = KeyboardActions.Default,
)

params.doc: the current phone number (digits only)

params.name: onValueChange
params.doc: callback invoked when the input changes

params.doc: the [Modifier] to apply

params.name: countryCode
params.doc: the country-code text shown in the leading slot,

params.name: flag
params.doc: optional composable rendered before [countryCode]; supply a

params.doc: optional field label — same semantics as [AurumTextField]

params.name: placeholder
params.doc: placeholder text — defaults to `"Phone Number"`

params.name: helperText
params.doc: assistive text below the field

params.name: errorText

params.name: successText
params.doc: success text — when non-null (and no error) the field

params.name: readOnly

params.doc: max number of digits accepted — defaults to `10`

params.name: keyboardActions
params.doc: IME action callbacks

components.intendedUse: Use whenever capturing a phone number that needs an E.164-style country prefix — login, KYC, recipient details. The leading country-code slot is always visible so users see the dialing context even before typing. For bare numeric capture without country context, use [AurumTextField] with a numeric keyboard.

do: Default `countryCode = "+91"` for India-first surfaces; pass an explicit value for international flows.
Pair with a country picker (when available) that updates `countryCode` + `flag` together — never let one drift from the other.
Use the `errorText` channel for "phone already registered" / "invalid number" feedback — keep the channel consistent with [AurumTextField].
dont: Bypass the country code — bare digit input loses E.164 context downstream and creates locale ambiguity.
Pre-format the value with spaces / hyphens; the underlying field stores raw digits and formatting belongs in display only.
Use this for OTP — [AurumOtpInput] is the right primitive for numeric one-time codes.

A phone-number-specific text field: country code + divider + digit input.
The SwiftUI port of aurum-android's `AurumPhoneNumberInput`.

Delegates to `AurumTextField` with a fixed leading slot that renders an
optional `flag` view, the `countryCode` text, and a thin vertical
divider. Defaults to a phone keyboard and a 10-digit max length; input
is silently filtered to digits only.

components.name: AurumSearchField
components.summary: A search-specific text field with a leading search icon and an optional trailing clear button.
components.signature:
@Composable
fun AurumSearchField(
    value: String,
    onValueChange: (String) -> Unit,
    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
    placeholder: String = "Search",
    enabled: Boolean = true,
    onClear: (() -> Unit)? = null,
)

params.name: value
params.doc: the current search query

params.name: onValueChange
params.doc: callback invoked when the query changes

params.name: modifier
params.doc: the [Modifier] to apply

params.name: placeholder
params.doc: placeholder text — defaults to "Search"

params.name: enabled
params.doc: whether the field accepts input

params.name: onClear
params.doc: optional callback invoked when the clear button is tapped;

components.intendedUse: Use as an in-screen search box at the top of a list / directory surface. The pill shape + leading magnifier glyph is the canonical "search" affordance — users recognise it instantly. For filtering by category use a row of [AurumChip]s instead; for free-form text input use [AurumTextField].

do: Wire `onValueChange` to a debounced query (250–400 ms) — search-as-you-type without flooding the backend.
Provide `onClear` whenever the search has any state worth clearing — users expect the X to wipe the query and reset results.
Anchor at the top of the scrollable surface; sticky-on-scroll if the list is long.
dont: Use as a generic single-line input — the pill shape signals search semantics; reusing it for unrelated text confuses users.
Submit on Enter without also surfacing real-time results; users expect search to feel live.
Stack two AurumSearchFields on the same surface — there should be one search context at a time.

)

ios.kdoc:
Aurum's search box — a pill-shaped `AurumTextField` with the canonical
leading magnifier and an optional clear affordance. The SwiftUI port of
aurum-android's `AurumSearchField`.

components.intendedUse: Use for free-text capture on form surfaces — name, address, notes, message body, anything that maps cleanly to a string. For specialised inputs reach for the dedicated component: [AurumOtpInput] for numeric one-time passwords, [AurumPhoneNumberInput] for phone numbers with country prefix, [AurumSearchField] for in-screen search boxes.

do: Pair `errorText` with [AurumColors.feedback]-tinted helper styling and a clear validation message — never silently mark fields wrong.
Set `maxLength` together with the character counter for any field with backend-enforced length limits — gives users instant feedback.
Use `KeyboardOptions(keyboardType = …)` to surface the right keyboard for the data shape (numeric, email, URL).
dont: Use this for OTP entry — [AurumOtpInput] handles paste, autofill, and resend-timer UX correctly.
Wrap `material3.OutlinedTextField` directly anywhere in feature code — its floating label and border notch contradict the Aurum anatomy.
Override `colors`/`shape` with raw values; recolour through validation state (`isError` / `isSuccess`) instead.

ios.kdoc:
Aurum's primary text-input field — the SwiftUI port of aurum-android's
`AurumTextField`.

Anatomy per Figma: an optional static `label` rendered **above** the
field (it never floats into the outline — Aurum deliberately opts out of
the Material floating-label behaviour), the outlined input box, and an
optional helper / error / success message below. The `placeholder` sits
inside the box whenever the value is empty and disappears as the user
types.

When `errorText` is non-nil the field enters the error state
automatically; `successText` (with no error) enters the success state.
Error / success / loading states show their trailing status affordance
automatically unless the caller supplies a `trailing` slot. A character
counter appears in the support row when `maxLength` is set; input beyond
it is truncated.

Platform notes vs Android: SwiftUI homes the caret at the end of
programmatically-set values natively (no `TextFieldValue` dance needed);
`readOnly` renders the display-only style and blocks editing via
`.disabled` (SwiftUI has no focusable-but-not-editable mode);
`isSecure: true` replaces Android's `PasswordVisualTransformation`.

    tint: Color = LocalContentColor.current.takeOrElse { Aurum.colors.surface.iconDefaultNormal },
)

components.intendedUse: Use to render any `AurumIcons.*` ImageVector with Aurum-token tinting. The 24 dp default matches Figma's canonical icon size; pass `Aurum.iconSize.*` tokens for other scales. For interactive tap targets wrap in [AurumIconButton]; for decorative inline glyphs inside text, render via [AurumText] with an inline icon span.

do: Provide a meaningful `contentDescription` whenever the icon conveys information not already present in nearby text — screen readers depend on it.
Use `Aurum.colors.surface.icon*` tokens for `tint` — they pair correctly with the surrounding background contrast.
Pick the `size` from `Aurum.iconSize.*` (sm / md / lg / xl / xxl); arbitrary dp values break the visual rhythm.
dont: Use raw drawable resources or `painterResource` directly — `AurumIcons.*` is the canonical entry point and round-trips with the manifest's icon catalog.
Tint with raw `Color()` literals; recolour through `Aurum.colors.*` so light/dark/state variants flow.
Wrap `material3.Icon` directly anywhere in feature code; that's exactly what this primitive prevents.

ios.kdoc:
Aurum's icon primitive — the SwiftUI port of aurum-android's `AurumIcon`.

Renders an `AurumIcons.*` asset at a configurable `size` (defaults to
`Aurum.iconSize.xl` = 24 pt, the canonical icon size in the Figma icon
library) with a tint. Assets are template-rendered, so the tint paints
the glyph's alpha mask — solid colours and gradients alike.

### Tint resolution order (mirrors Android's `LocalContentColor.takeOrElse`)
1. Explicit `tint:` parameter.
2. Ambient `\.aurumContentColor` — set by surfaces like `AurumButton`
for their leading/trailing slots.
3. `Aurum.colors.surface.iconDefaultNormal`.

components.name: AurumStepper
components.summary: Aurum's numeric stepper — a compact container with decrease/value/increase controls inside a single rounded container.
components.signature:
@Composable
fun AurumStepper(
    value: Int,
    onValueChange: (Int) -> Unit,
    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
    type: AurumStepperType = AurumStepperType.Neutral,
    minValue: Int = 0,
    maxValue: Int = Int.MAX_VALUE,
    enabled: Boolean = true,
)

params.name: value
params.doc: the current numeric value

params.name: onValueChange
params.doc: called with the new value when the user taps ±

params.name: modifier
params.doc: the [Modifier] to apply

params.name: type
params.doc: visual style — [AurumStepperType.Neutral] or [Brand]

params.name: minValue
params.doc: minimum allowed value (inclusive) — defaults to 0

params.name: maxValue
params.doc: maximum allowed value (inclusive)

params.name: enabled
params.doc: whether the stepper is interactive

components.intendedUse: Use for small bounded integer ranges where the user picks a count by incrementing — cart quantity, count of N selections, number of attendees. The "Initial State" / "Add" affordance handles the first-tap onboarding without the user needing to know the bounds. For free numeric input use [AurumTextField] with a numeric keyboard; for unbounded values use a slider (when available).

do: Set `maxValue` to the real backend limit so the + button disables on the boundary instead of erroring after submission.
Use `Brand` type when the stepper is the surface's CTA-equivalent (e.g. "add to cart" flows); `Neutral` for inline edit-existing-quantity rows.
Mirror the value back to the user with a clear running total / subtotal nearby — steppers shine when the consequence is visible.
dont: Use for free numeric capture — `AurumTextField` with a numeric keyboard is the right primitive when the value range is open.
Render multiple steppers in a row controlling the same backing value; one canonical control per quantity.
Skip the disabled state when the user hits `maxValue` — letting them tap and silently fail breaks the bound contract.

ios.kdoc:
Aurum's numeric stepper — a compact container with decrease/value/
increase controls inside a single rounded container. The SwiftUI port
of aurum-android's `AurumStepper` (Figma node `4506:201`).

When `value` equals `minValue` (typically 0), the stepper shows an
"Add" button instead of the ±/value layout, matching the Figma
"Initial State".

components.name: AurumGlobalHeader
components.summary: Aurum's global header — the persistent top bar used on every top-level destination (Home, Transactions, NEK, Rewards).
components.kdoc:
Aurum's global header — the persistent top bar used on every top-level
destination (Home, Transactions, NEK, Rewards). Mapped to the Figma
`Global Header` component (file `YvIxqu2oTlM6UnkL0OloHc`, node `5252:2274`).

This is a **scratch** component built with `Row` + `Box` from
`androidx.compose.foundation`. It does not wrap `material3.TopAppBar`.

Visual structure (left → right):
- Leading **profile pill** — 44 dp circular pill with a 1 dp
  `interactive/border/neutral/muted` border and a soft radial sheen.
  Accepts an [ImageVector] glyph via [profileIcon] (default
  `AurumIcons.User.User`) **or** any caller-supplied composable via
  [profileContent] for URL-loaded avatars.
- Right group — see [AurumGlobalHeaderRightContent]:
  - [AurumGlobalHeaderRightContent.Default]: optional Support pill (1 icon),
    optional Rewards+Notifications pill (2 icons + divider).
  - [AurumGlobalHeaderRightContent.LivePrice]: wider live-price pill on a
    dark surface, optional Rewards+Notifications pill.

Container: 360 dp wide reference, 68 dp tall (44 dp pills + 12 dp
vertical padding), `surface/background/page/base` background.

@param onProfileClick callback invoked when the leading profile pill is tapped.
@param rightContent the right-group content variant — see [AurumGlobalHeaderRightContent].
@param modifier the [Modifier] to apply to the root container.
@param profileIcon the [ImageVector] rendered in the profile pill when [profileContent] is `null`. Defaults to `AurumIcons.User.User`.
@param profileContent optional composable slot rendered inside the profile
  pill. When non-`null` overrides [profileIcon] — useful for URL-loaded
  avatars (e.g. via Glide / Coil at the call site).
@param containerColor the header background colour. Defaults to
  [Aurum.colors.surface.bgPageBase]. Pass [Color.Transparent] when the
  header sits on a separately-painted hero backdrop.
@param colorScheme switches the pill bg + icon + text tones between the
  default light treatment and the dark-glass treatment for hero zones.
  See [AurumGlobalHeaderColorScheme].

components.intendedUse: Use as the persistent top bar on every top-level destination — Home, Transactions, NEK, Rewards. The two [AurumGlobalHeaderRightContent] variants cover the design-system canonical right-side affordances; don't roll a custom header for these screens.

do: Choose the [AurumGlobalHeaderRightContent.LivePrice] variant only on screens where the live gold buy price is part of the screen's purpose (e.g. the home feed); use [AurumGlobalHeaderRightContent.Default] elsewhere.
Hoist the rewards / notifications click handlers up to your nav controller so the badge counts stay in sync with state changes.

Pass [profileContent] for URL-loaded avatars — the slot fills the pill's inner circle (inside the 1 dp border, clipped circular), so hand it a full-bleed image with `ContentScale.Crop`; don't size or clip it yourself. (The default [profileIcon] glyph path still renders at a 20 dp icon size.)
dont: Wrap `material3.TopAppBar` and tint it with Aurum colours — this component exists to prevent that anti-pattern.
Render two `AurumGlobalHeader`s in a single Composition — one per screen, anchored to the top inset.

Mix [AurumGlobalHeaderRightContent.LivePrice] with `null` rewards on a screen where users expect Rewards / Notifications access — the LivePrice variant intentionally allows hiding the rewards pill, but only do so on screens where those affordances are unambiguously available elsewhere.

Aurum's global header — the persistent top bar used on every top-level
destination (Home, Transactions, NEK, Rewards). The SwiftUI port of
aurum-android's `AurumGlobalHeader` (Figma node `5252:2274`).

Visual structure (left → right):
- Leading **profile pill** — 44 pt circular pill with a 1 pt
`interactive/border/neutral/muted` border and a soft radial sheen.

`AurumIcons.User.user`) **or** any caller-supplied view via the
`profileContent` slot for URL-loaded avatars.
- Right group — see `AurumGlobalHeaderRightContent`.

When the header touches the top screen edge, `containerColor` bleeds
under the status bar automatically while content stays clear — the
equivalent of Android's internal status-bar inset handling.

components.name: AurumNavBar
components.summary: Aurum's bottom navigation bar — the canonical "NavBar" component from the Figma design system (file YvIxqu2oTlM6UnkL0OloHc, component_set 5575:14226).
components.kdoc:
Aurum's bottom navigation bar — the canonical "NavBar" component from the
Figma design system (file `YvIxqu2oTlM6UnkL0OloHc`, component_set
`5575:14226`).

This is a **scratch** component built with `Row` + `Column` from
`androidx.compose.foundation`. It does not wrap `material3.NavigationBar`.

The Figma set defines four `layout` variants: `standard_3`, `standard_4`,
`standard_5`, and `center_qr_5`. Each cell — driven by the
[AurumNavBarItem] component_set `5575:14365` — renders a 24 dp icon (line
when unselected, filled when selected) plus a 10 sp label. Selected
cells render the icon with a `purple/500 → pink/50` linear gradient
tint and the label with `surface.textBrandNormal`; unselected cells
use `surface.textDefaultSubtle` for both.

**Minimum container width: 360 dp.** The design assumes a 360 dp +
Android baseline. At 320 dp the 5-cell math (64 dp/cell) clips the
trailing 1-2 chars of long labels (e.g. "Transactions") via
`TextOverflow.Clip`. That's a faithful preview of what real
Android-Go-class users would see; if you need full labels at 320 dp,
shorten them.

@param items the per-cell content; size must match `layout.itemCount`
@param selectedIndex the index of the currently-selected cell. For
  [AurumNavBarLayout.CenterQr5], this indexes into [items] — the centre
  QR button is *not* selectable and ignores this value.
@param onSelect callback invoked with the tapped cell index. For
  [AurumNavBarLayout.CenterQr5], indices match positions in [items]
  (0..3); the QR button has its own dedicated callback on the layout.
@param layout the bar's layout shape — see [AurumNavBarLayout]
@param modifier the [Modifier] to apply to the root container
@param containerColor the bar background colour. Defaults to
  [Aurum.colors.surface.bgPageBase].
components.signature:

    items: ImmutableList<AurumNavBarItem>,
    selectedIndex: Int,
    onSelect: (Int) -> Unit,
    layout: AurumNavBarLayout,
    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
    containerColor: Color = Aurum.colors.surface.bgPageBase,
)

params.doc: the index of the currently-selected cell. For

components.intendedUse: Use as the persistent bottom navigation primitive on top-level screens. Pick the layout variant that matches the destination count: 3 / 4 / 5 cells, or 4 cells flanking a centre Scan-QR action. Don't roll a custom bottom bar — this is the canonical surface.

do: Pass paired line/filled `AurumIcons.*` variants per item so the bar's selected-state contrast reads correctly (e.g. `AurumIcons.User.Home` + `AurumIcons.User.HomeFilled`).
Hoist `selectedIndex` in your screen state and route `onSelect` through your nav controller so the bar stays in sync with back-stack changes.
Match the layout to your top-level destination count — picking [Standard3]/[Standard4]/[Standard5] keeps spacing balanced; never partially-fill a 5-cell bar.
dont: Wrap `material3.NavigationBar` and tint it with Aurum colours — this component exists to prevent that anti-pattern.
Push more than 5 items into a layout — Android nav UX caps at 5; if you need more, restructure with sub-tabs or an overflow surface.
Render two `AurumNavBar`s in a single Composition — one per screen, anchored to the bottom inset.

android.kdoc:
Aurum's bottom navigation bar — the canonical "NavBar" component from the
Figma design system (file `YvIxqu2oTlM6UnkL0OloHc`, component_set
`5575:14226`).

This is a **scratch** component built with `Row` + `Column` from
`androidx.compose.foundation`. It does not wrap `material3.NavigationBar`.

The Figma set defines four `layout` variants: `standard_3`, `standard_4`,
`standard_5`, and `center_qr_5`. Each cell — driven by the
[AurumNavBarItem] component_set `5575:14365` — renders a 24 dp icon (line
when unselected, filled when selected) plus a 10 sp label. Selected
cells render the icon with a `purple/500 → pink/50` linear gradient
tint and the label with `surface.textBrandNormal`; unselected cells
use `surface.textDefaultSubtle` for both.

**Minimum container width: 360 dp.** The design assumes a 360 dp +
Android baseline. At 320 dp the 5-cell math (64 dp/cell) clips the
trailing 1-2 chars of long labels (e.g. "Transactions") via
`TextOverflow.Clip`. That's a faithful preview of what real
Android-Go-class users would see; if you need full labels at 320 dp,
shorten them.

@param items the per-cell content; size must match `layout.itemCount`
@param selectedIndex the index of the currently-selected cell. For
  [AurumNavBarLayout.CenterQr5], this indexes into [items] — the centre
  QR button is *not* selectable and ignores this value.
@param onSelect callback invoked with the tapped cell index. For
  [AurumNavBarLayout.CenterQr5], indices match positions in [items]
  (0..3); the QR button has its own dedicated callback on the layout.
@param layout the bar's layout shape — see [AurumNavBarLayout]
@param modifier the [Modifier] to apply to the root container
@param containerColor the bar background colour. Defaults to
  [Aurum.colors.surface.bgPageBase].

ios.kdoc:
Aurum's bottom navigation bar — the canonical "NavBar" from the Figma
design system (component set `5575:14226`). The SwiftUI port of
aurum-android's `AurumNavBar`.

Four `layout` variants: 3/4/5 evenly-spaced cells, or 4 cells flanking
a raised circular Scan-QR button. Each cell renders a 24 pt icon (line
unselected, filled selected) plus a label; selected cells tint the icon
with the `purple/500 → pink/50` brand ramp and the label with
`textBrandNormal`.

**Minimum container width: 360 pt.** The design assumes a 360 pt
baseline; below it, long labels ("Transactions") clip at the cell edge
— matching Figma's `overflow-clip` — rather than reflowing.

components.name: AurumPageHeader
components.summary: Aurum's Page Header — the canonical title-and-actions row at the top of a screen, from the Figma design system (file YvIxqu2oTlM6UnkL0OloHc, component_set 4826:1058).
components.kdoc:
Aurum's Page Header — the canonical title-and-actions row at the top of a
screen, from the Figma design system (file `YvIxqu2oTlM6UnkL0OloHc`,
component_set `4826:1058`).

This is a **scratch** component built with `Row` + `Box` from
`androidx.compose.foundation`. It does not wrap `material3.TopAppBar`.

The Figma set defines exactly 10 trailing-action `Type` variants —
each combination of `actionButton` (primary button), `icon1`, `icon2`,
and `isOverflow`:

| Type              | primaryButton | icon1 | icon2 | overflow |
|-------------------|:-------------:|:-----:|:-----:|:--------:|
| Default           |       —       |   —   |   —   |    —     |
| With Icon         |       —       |   ●   |   —   |    —     |
| With Icons        |       —       |   ●   |   ●   |    —     |
| With Overflow     |       —       |   —   |   —   |    ●     |
| 1icon+overflow    |       —       |   ●   |   —   |    ●     |
| icons+overflow    |       —       |   ●   |   ●   |    ●     |
| With Button       |       ●       |   —   |   —   |    —     |
| Button + Icon     |       ●       |   ●   |   —   |    —     |
| Button + Icons    |       ●       |   ●   |   ●   |    —     |
| Button + Overflow |       ●       |   —   |   —   |    ●     |

Plus two orthogonal instance properties: `backButton` (the leading back
icon) and `subtitle` (a second line under the title). Pass `null` for
any slot to omit it; the four trailing-action slots are positionally
meaningful, so e.g. `icon1 = null, icon2 = X` is invalid.

Layout (Figma):
- Container: full width, 56 dp tall, 16 dp horizontal padding,
  `surface/background/page/base` background
- Left group: optional back icon (32×40 dp wrapper, 24 dp icon) and
  title/subtitle column, separated by 12 dp; left group expands with
  `weight(1f)` and clips long titles via ellipsis
- Right group: 8 dp gap between trailing items, max width 140 dp,
  button capped at 120 dp wide × 32 dp tall

Typography (Figma 4826:1058):
- Title: `Title/S-SemiBold` (16/24, SemiBold 600)
- Subtitle: 12/16, Medium 500, primary family — same colour as title
  (hierarchy from weight + size only)
- Primary button label: `Label/L-Medium` (12/16, Medium 500); 36 dp tall
  pill with a 1 dp `interactive.borderPrimaryMuted` ring

@param title the page title; single-line with ellipsis overflow
@param modifier the [Modifier] to apply to the root container
@param subtitle optional subtitle text below the title; single-line
  with ellipsis overflow
@param onBack if non-null, the leading [backIcon] is displayed on the
  left and this callback is invoked on tap
@param backIcon glyph drawn in the leading slot when [onBack] is set.
  Defaults to [AurumIcons.Navigation.ChevronLeft] — the canonical back
  icon in the Figma component-set (4826:1086 → 5426:1363). Override
  sparingly, e.g. with `AurumIcons.Navigation.Close` for modal-style
  surfaces; the wrapper, touch target, and tint stay consistent.
@param primaryButton optional inline pill button on the right
@param icon1 optional first trailing action icon (Figma slot `icon1`)
@param icon2 optional second trailing action icon (Figma slot `icon2`)
@param overflow optional overflow icon (Figma slot `isOverflow`); always
  rendered after `icon1` / `icon2`
@param contentColor optional explicit override for the title/subtitle text
  colour. [Color.Unspecified] (default) defers to [colorScheme]; pass a
  concrete colour only to override the scheme's text tone. Icon tints always
  follow [colorScheme].
@param containerColor the bar background colour. Defaults to
  [Aurum.colors.surface.bgPageBase]; pass [Color.Transparent] with
  [colorScheme] = [AurumPageHeaderColorScheme.OnDark] for a hero backdrop.
@param colorScheme the on-light / on-dark tone set for icons + text — see
  [AurumPageHeaderColorScheme]. Defaults to [AurumPageHeaderColorScheme.OnLight].
components.signature:
@Composable
fun AurumPageHeader(
    title: String,
    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
    subtitle: String? = null,
    onBack: (() -> Unit)? = null,
    backIcon: ImageVector = AurumIcons.Navigation.ChevronLeft,
    primaryButton: AurumPageHeaderButton? = null,
    icon1: AurumPageHeaderAction? = null,
    icon2: AurumPageHeaderAction? = null,
    overflow: AurumPageHeaderAction? = null,
    contentColor: Color = Color.Unspecified,
    containerColor: Color = Aurum.colors.surface.bgPageBase,
    colorScheme: AurumPageHeaderColorScheme = AurumPageHeaderColorScheme.OnLight,
    /**
     * When true (default),
    the top bar extends behind the status bar:
     * the [containerColor] fills under the status bar,
    and the content
     * (title,
    icons) is padded below it. Set to false if the caller
     * handles status bar insets externally.
     *
     * This is the edge-to-edge behaviour recommended by Google for
     * Android 15+ and the default for [AurumScreen].
     */
    applyStatusBarInsets: Boolean = true,
)
